title: Manga Viz release
wikitext:
[]: [Manga] Viz release             Quote Post by Aoren » Sat May 30, 2015 8:44 pm I would like the Viz release of the manga. First off, is it complete? If not up to where? In how many parts is each book divided? How much should I spend and how hard is it to find (complete)?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat May 30, 2015 9:04 pm It is complete. Viz Media collected volumes 1 through 12 in 4 huge omnibuses, and then they separately printed the late two volumes as they were released. You can start your Eva manga quest here.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun May 31, 2015 8:43 am Some of the individual volumes are very hard to find, so start with the 3 in 1 omnibus releases.

[]:             Quote Post by Strings » Sun May 31, 2015 4:21 pm I can sell you 1-8 and 14 if you're interested. $60 + shipping :) I'll even throw in the Sadamoto Evangelion Der Mond art book free with purchase! :)

[]:             Quote Post by FullMetalBiscuit » Mon Jun 01, 2015 5:29 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Some of the individual volumes are very hard to find, so start with the 3 in 1 omnibus releases.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jun 01, 2015 5:35 am Vol 9 specifically is in very short supply. But the omnibus volumes are printed that bit bigger, and have colour plates as well - they're the better bet really. I just hope they do a matching version of the last two volumes (though note that the spines of the four omnibus volumes make a complete matching pattern at the top...).

[]:             Quote Post by cody727kirby » Mon Jun 01, 2015 9:21 am Volume 9 has had a reprint since then. It's available for a reasonable price again on Amazon :) http://www.amazon.com/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-Vol-9/dp/1591167078

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jun 01, 2015 9:22 am ^^ Nothing that can’t extend by typing the last two volumes to read “| END OF” at the top, making a whole set read “EVANGELION | END OF.” Even if they decide to just leave it blank, I’d totally buy the larger, colored editions despite already having the regular printed editions.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jun 01, 2015 9:27 am Oh, sure! And I hope that's what they'll do, sooner rather than later. Now we can discuss what they should add to make the volume the same thickness as the others!

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jun 01, 2015 9:29 am All of the interviews from all of the Eva staff ever.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Jun 01, 2015 1:14 pm Monkey and I both figured it's a matter of time that 13 + 14 + bonus content will get its own VIZ omnibus volume one of these days. (Personally hoping for Carl Horn's thoughts on the last 2 volumes!) Good point that it could read "The End of" on it to flow with "Neon Genesis Evangelion".

[]:             Quote Post by FullMetalBiscuit » Mon Jun 01, 2015 3:22 pm Slightly off topic; I just got volumes 1-7 in a bundle and they don't have white spines and are slightly larger than the usual ones. Whats the deal with them?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jun 01, 2015 3:28 pm There have been multiple editions; sounds as if you've got the earlier version, which came in two forms: flipped, and then later in similar covers unflipped and labelled something like "special edition". Then they were reprinted unflipped in the slightly smaller format, in 2004 as I recall, and the remaining volumes were issued in that new format. So your set of seven is all there are of the older format. I got a couple of the old ones before I understood the difference.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Mon Jun 01, 2015 11:27 pm @FullMetalBiscut: Agreed. You have the earliest editions to come out, though I'm not sure if they're flipped or the "Special Edition" which were unflipped (unflipped manga was still a novelty back then). In fact, I have volumes 1-3 in the special edition, but it looked as if the spines were loosening, so I got the first omnibus to replace them. I'll admit, it looks good.

[]:             Quote Post by FullMetalBiscuit » Tue Jun 02, 2015 12:02 pm Ah, that explains why volume 4 is flipped but the rest aren't. Thanks for the info. A little annoying as I really only got them for display purposes with the rest of my white spine ones Oh well, was only £15 and I guess it's another version of the manga to own.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Jun 02, 2015 3:23 pm cody727kirby wrote: Volume 9 has had a reprint since then.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jun 02, 2015 11:38 pm I don’t care if Vol. 9 got a reprint. The 3-1 omnibus just looks better due to the larger images. And since it’s back in print, the rarity factor has been taken away from it anyway. I personally don’t see a point in buying it.
